comments.body:
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough The dashboard HTML template is restructured with panels reordered and new panels added. RUN PROGRESS, PENDING ORDERS, and auto-refreshing SUMMARY blocks are introduced, followed by reorganization of POSITIONS, EQUITY CURVE, VS SPY, and WATCHLIST panels. All changes are presentational and concern template layout and fragment loading. ## Changes **Dashboard Panel Reorganization** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**New Interactive Panels**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/templates/dashboard.html`|RUN PROGRESS panel added with live queue and cancel features; PENDING ORDERS panel added with approval and execute controls.| |**Auto-refresh Configuration**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/templates/dashboard.html`|SUMMARY block configured to auto-refresh via HTMX with hx-get/hx-trigger directives.| |**Panel Reorganization**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/templates/dashboard.html`|POSITIONS, EQUITY CURVE, VS SPY, and WATCHLIST panels reordered with refresh intervals (10s, 60s, default, and 30s respectively).| ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 2 (Simple) | ⏱️ ~12 minutes ## Poem > A rabbit hops through panels new, > Rearranging the dashboard view— > RUN PROGRESS, ORDERS in their place, > Auto-refresh at a sprightly pace! 🐰✨ > The dashboard dances, fresh and bright, > Every refresh feels just right.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 5
✅ Passed checks (5 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title clearly and specifically describes the main change: reordering dashboard panels to prioritize user intent, which aligns with the changeset's primary objective. | | Description check | ✅ Passed | The description includes all required sections from the template: Summary, Type, Changes, Testing, and Checklist, with comprehensive details about the rationale and panel reorganization. | | Docstring Coverage | ✅ Passed | No functions found in the changed files to evaluate docstring coverage. Skipping docstring coverage check. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `refactor/dashboard-panel-order`
--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands and usage tips.

reviews.body:
🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
src/agentic_portfolio/web/templates/dashboard.html (1)
`49-106`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Add an order assertion in the web view test to prevent regressions.** This PR’s behavior change is ordering, but current tests only validate presence. A simple index-order assertion will lock in the intent and catch accidental reordering later.
✅ Suggested test diff (example) ```diff diff --git a/tests/test_web_views.py b/tests/test_web_views.py @@ def test_dashboard_panels_present(client): html = client.get("/").text for name in [ "RUN PROGRESS", "PENDING ORDERS", "SUMMARY", "POSITIONS", "EQUITY CURVE", "VS SPY", "RECENT ORDERS", "DECISIONS", "WATCHLIST", "SYSTEM", ]: assert name in html + +def test_dashboard_panel_order(client): + html = client.get("/").text + expected_order = [ + "RUN PROGRESS", + "PENDING ORDERS", + "SUMMARY", + "POSITIONS", + "EQUITY CURVE", + "VS SPY", + "RECENT ORDERS", + "DECISIONS", + "WATCHLIST", + "SYSTEM", + ] + positions = [html.index(label) for label in expected_order] + assert positions == sorted(positions) ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@src/agentic_portfolio/web/templates/dashboard.html` around lines 49 - 106, The test for the dashboard web view currently only asserts presence of order items but not their sequence; update the existing dashboard/orders web-view test (the test that hits the fragment rendered into elements like the "pending-panel-body", "orders" or "positions" fragments) to include an explicit index/order assertion — e.g., after fetching the HTML for the fragment (hx-get target like "pending-panel-body" or the "/fragment/orders" response) parse the returned items and assert their expected order by index (first item equals X, second equals Y) so future regressions that change ordering are caught. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Nitpick comments: In `@src/agentic_portfolio/web/templates/dashboard.html`: - Around line 49-106: The test for the dashboard web view currently only asserts presence of order items but not their sequence; update the existing dashboard/orders web-view test (the test that hits the fragment rendered into elements like the "pending-panel-body", "orders" or "positions" fragments) to include an explicit index/order assertion — e.g., after fetching the HTML for the fragment (hx-get target like "pending-panel-body" or the "/fragment/orders" response) parse the returned items and assert their expected order by index (first item equals X, second equals Y) so future regressions that change ordering are caught. ```
